purpose: Propose a production acceptance threshold framework for player_history_production_feature_v0, per PR #124 section 5 prerequisite 7, which every prior document in the chain has deferred. Threshold-design/report only; does not authorize production binding, a leakage audit execution, or model wiring.
decision_rationale: The quantitative threshold components (see quantitative_threshold_components) are well-supported by the replicated evidence and proposed as concrete candidates, not left open-ended. But acceptance from current evidence alone is not justified: the evidence is one target season (2025), observed under two source-governance regimes that produced an IDENTICAL result (delta=0 vs #112 on every arm) rather than two independent seasons of validation. PR #124 section 5 prerequisite 7 requires either an additional season of validation or an explicit, reviewed risk acceptance before a threshold may rely on single-season evidence alone; this document does not make that risk-acceptance case. The candidate framework is therefore reviewable and amendable, pending additional validation, not binding on any future leakage-audit or wiring proposal yet.

replicated_joined_population_metrics.candidate_vs_promoted_delta_note: identical (delta=0) on every arm per the #122 candidate-source comparison; strong replication under two source-governance regimes, but the same underlying 2025-target population, not two independent seasons

quantitative_threshold_components.rationale: Set well below the observed 41.9% so a future season with normal signal decay still passes; must be beaten together with the shuffled-control component (never baseline alone).
Preserves the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing required by every prior gate in this chain; beating baseline without also beating the shuffled control by a margin is not acceptable.

~20% headroom above the observed value, set well below both baseline (68.926) and shuffled control (72.031), so a season where the signal degrades meaningfully still fails even if it nominally still beats both comparators.
~19% headroom above the observed value, set well below both baseline (88.553) and shuffled control (90.409).
RMSE guardrail alongside the MAE-primary metric, consistent with the secondary-metric check already used in the #121/#122 decision rule.

quantitative_threshold_components.candidate_threshold: full feature set adopted only if its relative joined-MAE improvement over production_only exceeds 2%, AND the PR #128 section 2.2 governance test passes (no expanded forbidden-field surface; reviewed as its own MINOR-or-larger contract amendment)
quantitative_threshold_components.rationale: Gives PR #128 section 2.2's previously-undefined 'meaningful bar' a concrete number for the first time. The observed 0.35% gap does not clear it, so production_only remains the v0 default scope.

quantitative_threshold_components.candidate_threshold: must be reported (count/share/by-position) on every evaluation; share > 35% of evaluated population requires additional justification before threshold evaluation is considered valid
quantitative_threshold_components.rationale: A soft ceiling, not a hard reject at current evidence: 20.5% is well under it. Exists to prevent a future evaluation on a thin joined subset from silently passing a threshold that was calibrated on a much richer joined population.

single_season_risk.statement: The current replicated evidence (#112 -> #122) is strong but centered on one target season (2025), observed under two different source-governance regimes (candidate, then promoted) that produced an IDENTICAL result (delta=0 on every arm) rather than two independent seasons of validation. This document does NOT make an explicit, reviewed case for why one replicated season is sufficient, per PR #124 section 5 prerequisite 7's requirement. Making that case without a second season of actual evidence would be exactly the failure mode that prerequisite warns against.

qualitative_governance_conditions_scope_: These conditions gate a future PRODUCTION WIRING/BINDING proposal (one that would actually touch seasonalPprModel.ts or a production feature path), not the leakage-audit design/execution issue itself. The next_allowed_issue (additional-validation design) and a later leakage-audit design issue are exactly where the leakage-audit condition below gets satisfied -- it cannot be a precondition for opening the issue that satisfies it, only for the wiring proposal that would come after.
qualitative_governance_conditions: source identity lock and committed-mirror source-identity verification (PR #130) must pass for whatever artifact snapshot a future proposal uses, re-verified at proposal time
the contract instance must pass structural validation (schema, closed enums, verbatim non-advice/non-ranking statement, null semantics, forbidden-field scan) per PR #130's validator
the deterministic replay/validation command must reproduce its own pinned smoke metrics exactly; a mismatch invalidates the evidence regardless of what the metrics show
the missing-history subgroup must be reported (count/share/by-position) for the specific population any future proposal evaluates, never silently passed through

a production-path leakage audit (PR #124 section 5 prerequisite 4; PR #128 section 2.5's checklist), broader than the experimental leakage discipline already enforced, must have been separately designed and executed against the specific production inference path being proposed, with a passing result -- clearing the quantitative threshold does not substitute for this audit

human sign-off (PR #128 section 2.8) must be a comment or review on the specific wiring PR, dated, attributable to the repository's merge-authority operator, and must reference the exact run_id/contract_version being wired, confirmation the leakage audit passed for that specific proposal, and the specific threshold evidence relied on
no Fantasy/product consumer, ranking, or advice behavior is authorized by threshold acceptance alone -- consumer approval remains its own separate, explicitly-approved review
non_negotiables_preserved: target season S may only use seasons < S -- no exceptions
no target-season or partial-target-season substitution, under any feature-availability fallback
missing-history players must not be silently zero-filled -- entire feature block stays null, and its presence must be reported
source artifact identity must be path + sha256 + promotion review, never sha256 alone; mismatch at generation time fails closed
the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled validation framing remains required for any future evaluation of this signal
this document does not authorize seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
any Fantasy/product consumer requires its own separate, explicitly-approved review
this document does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact

next_allowed_issue.title: Design additional validation for player-history threshold acceptance
next_allowed_issue.scope_note: May design (not necessarily execute in the same PR) a second-season or backtest validation approach for the player-history signal, using the same real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing, governed-source discipline, and non-production contract infrastructure from PR #130. Must not jump to a production-path leakage audit design/execution issue or a seasonalPprModel.ts wiring issue -- those remain gated behind an accepted threshold, which this document does not grant.
forbidden_in_next_issue: seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
production-path leakage audit execution against a concrete wiring proposal

non_goals: does not accept a production acceptance threshold as binding
does not run a production-path leakage audit
does not modify seasonalPprModel.ts or any other production Forecast file
does not add player-history features to any production model
does not approve a Fantasy consumer, UI surface, ranking, or advice behavior
does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact
does not claim the replicated result is stable across future seasons or a production-scale rolling retraining cadence

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: setting a threshold that only today's exact numbers can pass
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: quantitative_threshold_components are set with deliberate headroom below observed values
relative_mae_improvement_over_baseline and _over_shuffled_control both required, preserving the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: accepting a threshold from one replicated season without acknowledgment
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: single_season_risk.accepted is explicitly false with a stated rationale

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: letting the full feature-family set in because it is marginally better
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: production_only_vs_full_feature_set_added_value_bar gives PR #128 section 2.2's bar a concrete number; the observed 0.35% gap does not clear it

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: treating threshold proposal as authorization for a leakage audit or wiring
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: qualitative_governance_conditions and next_allowed_issue state the leakage audit remains separate and this document does not accept the threshold at all

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: silently passing through a shrinking or growing no-history subgroup
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: no_history_subgroup_reporting_ceiling sets a soft ceiling and requires the subgroup be reported for whatever population any future evaluation targets

next_step: The decision permits opening a SEPARATE 'Design additional validation for player-history threshold acceptance' issue (see next_allowed_issue). It does not permit a production-path leakage audit design/execution issue or a seasonalPprModel.ts wiring issue; those remain gated behind an accepted threshold, which this document does not grant.
